args.query:
SELECT "Index",
SUM(CASE WHEN "Close" > "Open" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS up_days,
SUM(CASE WHEN "Close" < "Open" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS down_days,
SUM(CASE WHEN "Close" = "Open" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS flat_days
FROM index_trade
WHERE "Date" >= '2018-01-01' AND "Date" <= '2018-12-31' AND "Index" IN ('GSPTSE','IXIC','NYA')
GROUP BY "Index";

exec_args.sql:
SELECT "Index",
SUM(CASE WHEN "Close" > "Open" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS up_days,
SUM(CASE WHEN "Close" < "Open" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS down_days,
SUM(CASE WHEN "Close" = "Open" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS flat_days
FROM index_trade
WHERE "Date" >= '2018-01-01' AND "Date" <= '2018-12-31' AND "Index" IN ('GSPTSE','IXIC','NYA')
GROUP BY "Index";

result.preview:
"Among North American indices in the dataset, GSPTSE (Toronto) and IXIC (NASDAQ) had more up days than down days in 2018.

Counts (2018):
- GSPTSE: 44 up days, 41 down days, 1 flat day
- IXIC: 44 up days, 31 down days, 0 flat days
- NYA: 36 up days, 42 down days, 0 flat days (had more down days)"

- IXIC: 44 up days, 31 down days, 0 flat days
- NYA: 36 up days, 42 down days, 0 flat days (had more down days)
